name: Delaware Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Delaware - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Speech/Language Pathologists, Audiologists and Hearing Aid Dispensers

address.streetAddress: 861 Silver Lake Blvd., Suite 203, Dover, DE 19904
address.description: Cannon Building, 861 Silver Lake Blvd., Suite 203, Dover, DE 19904

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Delaware SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost is approximately $141. Biennial renewal is $111.

mainEntity.name: Is Delaware part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Delaware has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

mainEntity.name: Does Delaware allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Delaware permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

mainEntity.name: Does Delaware have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Delaware participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Delaware without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Delaware?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Delaware while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
